notes: Sub 5 (T-scaled mpnet) + a SINGLE shrunk global mean-residual offset from K=5 labels per category. p_final = clip(p_sub5 - clip(mean_residual * n/(n+lambda), -0.05, 0.05)). Sub 8 (per-subject offset) lost 0.10 NLL because 25 labels across ~5 subjects gave noisy per-subject estimates; this variant aggregates all 25 into a single global shift, shrinks it 50%% with lambda=25, and caps magnitude at 5pp. Slide 34: adaptive labels for 'light per-run calibration; not training'. NO labeling.py shipped so the platform supplies random labels (slide 34: 'random fallback if missing') -- random gives an unbiased mean-residual estimator; the uncertainty*diversity acquisition would bias sampling toward hard cases.
